title: 6 Logging in to ORS
body_text:
6	Logging in to ORS
6.1	Overview of Logging in to ORS
Access to ORS is granted through password authentication on Central 1âs Client Centre. To access ORS, the individual must have a username set up on Client Centre. The Client Centre username must be assigned access to the ORS application. See Section 5.2, Creating ORS Users, for procedures.
6.2	Logging in to ORS
Proceed as follows to log in to ORS:
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
. Enter your username and password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 1.
Note: 	Youâll be prompted to enter your username before this page appears if itâs your first login to the Client Centre, youâve deleted your cookies, or youâre using incognito/private browsing.
Figure 1: Client Centre Login
Select
Applications.
See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Accessing Applications on the Client Centre
Select
ORS (Online Return System)
See Figure 3.
Note: 	Applications are grouped by type (e.g., Admin & Support, Payments). If needed, scroll down the page to find the application. If youâve saved the application as a favourite, it will also appear under âMy Favouritesâ.
Figure 3: Accessing ORS
Important
:	Always log off the Client Centre and close your browser when you step away from your desk or when you are finished using the system.
6.3	ORS Alerts & Holidays Page
The Alerts & Holidays page displays messages from Central 1 regarding ORS, followed by a two-year calendar showing Payments Canada holidays when cheques and AFTs wonât be processed. For example, if images for a specific clearing region arenât available, a message advising financial institutions of the delay will be posted. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Alerts & Holidays Page
6.4	Key Navigational Links on ORS
ORS menu options are based on ORS usage level(s) and ORS user type. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Alerts & Holidays Page
Note:	Donât use the Refresh button in your browser as this will return you to the Alerts & Holidays page. To refresh a page, right-click and select
Refresh
from the menu.
END
